title: Microsoft debuts Surface Pro and Surface Laptop with new jade green color and Qualcomm Snapdragon X2 chips refreshed devices start at $1,499 with 16GB of RAM
summary: Microsoft updates Surface Pro and Surface Laptop with Snapdragon X2 chips, haptic feedback, and a new jade green color. The refreshed devices start at $1,499 with 16GB of RAM. This update aims to enhance user experience and performance.
insight: Microsoft refreshes Surface Pro and Laptop with modern, high-end features

title: MSI MPG Coreliquid P22 360 Review: Low noise, strong performance, budget price
summary: The MSI MPG Coreliquid P22 360 is a budget-friendly AIO liquid cooler with strong thermal performance and a 2.1-inch IPS display. It's paired with AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D CPU to test its efficiency. This AIO offers a low noise level and impressive performance at an affordable price.
insight: Budget-friendly AIO liquid cooler with high thermal performance capabilities.

title: AGI AI858 2TB SSD Review High-end PCIe 5 speeds on a budget
summary: The AGI AI858 offers high-end PCIe 5.0 speeds at a budget-friendly price, featuring good random read latency and a bundled heatsink. This makes it a great entry point for users seeking high-performance storage without breaking the bank. The AGI AI858 is an attractive option for those who want to upgrade their storage without making significant trade-offs.
insight: Affordable high-speed storage for gamers and content creators

title: Intel's one-two punch plan in desktop CPUs is taking shape Z990 spotted, Nova Lake detailed, Raptor Lake Next teased
summary: Intel's next-gen desktop CPU plans are taking shape with several new models. The Z990 and Nova Lake have been spotted, and Raptor Lake Next has been teased. These updates suggest a significant shift in Intel's desktop CPU strategy.
insight: Intel's evolving plans may shake up the desktop CPU market dynamics

title: Commodore announces Linux-based flip phone with no social media, no browser the Callback 8020 will be available in five retro colorways starting at $499, runs 99% of Android apps
summary: Commodore unveiled a retro-styled flip phone, the Callback 8020, with Linux-based OS and Android app support, available in five retro colorways starting at $499. It features no social media and no browser, appealing to those seeking a minimalist mobile experience.
insight: Nostalgic consumers seek low-tech, high-security flip phone with Android app support options.

title: Save 40% on this ultra-fast Samsung microSD card with 256GB storage, now just $47.99 $32 saving on P9 Express model with read speeds of up to 800 MB/s, perfect for a gaming laptop or a Nintendo Switch 2
summary: Save $32 on a Samsung P9 Express microSD card with 256GB storage and read speeds up to 800MB/s, perfect for gaming laptops or the Nintendo Switch 2.
insight: Enhanced gaming performance with ultra-fast storage

title: Get an RTX 5090 gaming laptop for less than $3,000 Acer's 16-inch Predator with 2TB of storage and 32GB of RAM is now $500 off
summary: Save $500 on Acer's 16-inch Predator Helios AI gaming laptop with RTX 5090 graphics and a 240Hz OLED screen. The discounted price is $2,999. This powerful gaming laptop features 2TB storage and 32GB RAM.
insight: High-end gaming laptops become more affordable with significant price cuts.

title: These are the four keyboards I'm still using after reviewing keyboards for years How a 500 Hz TKL and a Stream Deck layout dominated my desk
summary: The author has found four keyboards that meet their needs for writing and gaming, despite the elusive perfect board. These keyboards offer a combination of features that make them stand out, including a 500 Hz TKL and Stream Deck layout. The author has been unable to find a better alternative.
insight: Finding the right keyboard can elevate user experience significantly

title: AMD s massive SP7 socket for EPYC Venice and Intel s gargantuan 9,324-pin socket for Diamond Rapids appear at Computex SP7 and LGA9324-1 sockets will power the next generation of AI servers
summary: AMD and Intel unveil massive new sockets for next-gen AI servers, supporting 16 DDR5 memory channels.
insight: Increased memory capacity drives AI server performance advancements.

title: Finland charges Russian captain and crew member of ship suspected of damaging undersea cables prosecutors claim ship had eight more targets before it was stopped by coast guard
summary: Finnish prosecutors charge two crew members for damaging undersea cables, with two more detained.
insight: Damage to undersea cables poses significant risks to global internet connectivity.

title: TSMC says panel packaging won't replace CoWoS anytime soon for the largest future AI processors wafer-level tech can scale to 58 massive dies in one package
summary: TSMC's Kevin Zhang says wafer-level packaging is more advanced than panel-level packaging, with the ability to scale to 58 massive dies in one package, making it more suitable for large future AI processors.
insight: Wafer-level packaging outpaces panel-level for massive AI chip integration.

title: Intel's long-lost data center prototype 'Arctic Sound' Xe-HP multi-tile GPU surfaces in new engineering sample Company's cancelled AI processor features 32GB of HBM2E
summary: Intel's cancelled 'Arctic Sound' AI GPU prototype features 2 tiles and 32GB of HBM2E memory. It's based on the original Xe-HP architecture, showcasing the company's early AI processor developments. The engineering sample reveals a powerful GPU design.
insight: Early glimpse into Intel's abandoned AI GPU design advancements.

title: Amazon UK has slashed 43% off this excellent 4K 32-inch OLED gaming monitor lowest-ever price on this ASUS ROG Strix OLED now just 599
summary: Amazon UK offers a discounted price for the ASUS ROG Strix OLED gaming monitor, with 43% off its original price. The 32-inch 4K display is now available for £599. This is considered a lowest-ever price for this model.
insight: Exceptional gaming performance at an unbeatable price point available

title: Project Jupiter AI data center build raises concerns about water usage in rural New Mexico desert Oracle calls water usage 'negligible' for 11 million gallon one-time fill
summary: Oracle's Project Jupiter data center in New Mexico raises water usage concerns despite the company's assurance of 'negligible' impact. The facility, which will hold an 11 million-gallon one-time fill, is located in a rural desert area already struggling with water consumption. Residents are skeptical about the data center's water usage claims.
insight: Water scarcity in desert regions demands careful data center planning and management.

title: Enthusiast hacks Valve s AMD-first gaming OS to run on Intel hardware SteamOS boots on Intel Arc B580 desktop GPU, but it takes a Radeon card, installer workaround, and Resizable BAR fix
summary: A Reddit user successfully installed SteamOS on an Intel Arc B580 GPU, requiring a Radeon-assisted install workaround and Resizable BAR fix to achieve performance. The early proof of concept demonstrates SteamOS can run on Intel hardware, potentially broadening compatibility options.
insight: SteamOS runs on Intel hardware with creative workarounds and tweaks applied

title: AMD takes over MEXT for memory tiering tech that enables flash to appear as DRAM to applications tech to 'address growing memory constraints' in the data center
summary: AMD acquires MEXT to improve memory efficiency with Predictive Memory Engine. This technology offloads infrequently accessed data from DRAM to NAND storage. This addresses growing memory constraints in the data center.
insight: Offloading infrequent data from DRAM saves memory resources.

title: Multiple small Tennessee counties pass temporary data center bans Nashville also passed near-unanimous moratorium on first reading
summary: Tennessee towns are passing data center moratoriums to delay projects. The temporary bans have gained support in rural regions. Nashville also voted on a near-unanimous moratorium.
insight: Rural communities push back against massive data center developments.

title: Marvell details vision of optically-interconnected data centers spanning across thousands of kilometers new interconnects sampling later this year would allow CSPs to pool resources based on workload
summary: Marvell envisions optically interconnected data centers spanning thousands of kilometers, enabling CSPs to pool resources based on workload. The company already has the necessary hardware to build these interconnected data centers. This technology could revolutionize data center connectivity.
insight: Enhanced resource pooling and workload optimization for CSPs

title: $4,000 AMD Ryzen AI Halo Developer Platform features 126 TOPS Ryzen AI Max+ 395 processor
summary: AMD's Ryzen AI Halo Developer Platform offers 126 TOPS performance and 16 cores for AI and other applications.
insight: Advances AI computing for developers with high-performance and power-efficient Ryzen processor.

title: ADLINK COM-HPC-mPTL COM-HPC Mini computer-on-module features up to 180 TOPS Intel Core Ultra Series 3 CPU
summary: The ADLINK COM-HPC-mPTL is a high-performance COM-HPC mini computer-on-module with up to 180 TOPS, powered by the Intel Core Ultra Series 3 processor. It supports up to 64GB LPDDR5x memory and features multiple display interfaces, PCIe lanes, and a 40-pin debug connector. The module is designed for demanding applications in various industries.
insight: High-performance computing for AI, ML, and edge applications.

title: OneThingCloud decentralized bandwidth sharing/distributed CDN solution pays you for your spare bandwidth
summary: OneThingCloud allows users to monetize their spare bandwidth by installing a small hardware device at home. The device, known as the OneThing Edge Cube, joins a decentralized network to cache and distribute content, earning users passive income. This solution is based on the Rockchip RK3566 SoC and has multiple variants with varying specifications.
insight: Monetize spare bandwidth with a low-maintenance, decentralized hardware solution

title: Best of Sensors: Siargo wins with MEMS Mass Flow Sensor
summary: The Siargo MEMS mass flow sensor FS4000 won Best Instrumentation at Best of Sensors 2026 for its dynamic thermal transfer data capture capabilities. This sensor excels in heat distribution and time measurements. Its innovative technology has made it a standout in the industry.
insight: Accurate thermal monitoring enables precise temperature control and efficiency

title: Sensors Converge panel sees more credible Edge AI apps emerging
summary: Smart glasses with neural networks detect early onset of epileptic seizures, citing growth in Edge AI applications.
insight: Edge AI allows for real-time, on-device health monitoring and intervention.

title: EA launches new platform to put even more ads inside games
summary: EA launches EA Advertising platform to integrate ads into games.
insight: Brands increase reach through immersive gaming experiences.

title: The rise of "dopamine sites": fake food delivery and shopping apps that satisfy cravings without spending a cent
summary: Young people in South Korea visit 'dopamine sites' for instant gratification, often browsing fake food delivery and shopping apps to satisfy cravings without spending money.
insight: Instant gratification fuels addictive online behaviors in South Korean youth.

title: Xbox is closing Ninja Theory, and Double Fine and Compulsion may not be far behind
summary: Xbox is reportedly closing Ninja Theory, leaving staff to find a buyer.
insight: Potential closure of key game development studios impacts industry stability.

title: The Best Phones for Every Budget - 2026 Update
summary: The best phone for you in 2026 can be found across various budgets. From affordable options under $200 to flagship devices over $1,000, there's a strong case for every price point. Whether you prioritize budget-friendliness, performance, or features, 2026 has a phone for you.
insight: Every budget has a great phone to match your needs and preferences.

title: Intel CPUs with Nvidia RTX integrated graphics are targeting an early 2028 release
summary: Intel's x86 processors with Nvidia RTX graphics are expected to release in the first quarter of 2028, potentially challenging AMD's high-end mobile APUs and boosting both companies' platform ambitions.
insight: Nvidia RTX integration boosts Intel's mobile computing competitiveness significantly.

title: The FBI built a fake town in Alabama to study and simulate real-world cyberattacks
summary: The FBI built a simulated town in Alabama to study real-world cyberattacks. The Cyber Range is a training facility with wired systems mimicking a community network. It's designed to prepare for and respond to cyber threats.
insight: Real-world cyberattack simulations help improve response and defense strategies.

title: Nothing CEO warns memory costs now exceed 50% of smartphone's hardware bill
summary: Nothing CEO Carl Pei warns that memory costs now exceed 50% of smartphone hardware bill due to rising AI component prices.
insight: Soaring AI demands drive up memory costs, impacting consumer device affordability.

title: Google Earth just turned your browser into a flight simulator
summary: Google Earth now features a flight simulator on its web platform, allowing users to experience flight without a download. To access it, users can hit Explore Earth, then Tools > Flight Simulator. The simulator comes with two aircraft options: the F-16 and the Cirrus SR22.
insight: Interactive web-based flight simulator offers immersive real-time experiences.

title: PCB prices are up 40% in a month because of a material most people have never heard of
summary: PCB prices have surged 40% in a month due to supply chain disruptions caused by the conflict in the Middle East, specifically a shortage of a crucial material. The shortage stems from damage to a key plant in Jubail, and the risk of cargo disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. This has a significant impact on industries reliant on printed circuit boards.
insight: Global electronics supply chains are vulnerable to Middle Eastern conflicts.

title: Fox to acquire Roku in $22 billion deal, its largest ever
summary: Fox Corporation is acquiring Roku in a $22 billion deal, paying $160 per share. The deal will see existing Fox shareholders own 73% and Roku shareholders own 27% of the combined company. The transaction is expected to close soon.
insight: Significant consolidation in the streaming media and entertainment industry landscape

title: Files app brings smarter folder size scanning to its already capable Windows file manager
summary: The Files app now features on-demand folder size calculation, improving performance and preserving file selections when switching layouts.
insight: Faster, more efficient Windows file management for improved productivity

title: Onimusha: Way of the Sword releases September 25 with surprisingly modest system requirements
summary: Onimusha: Way of the Sword releases after nearly two decades of silence, reviving the mainline series. It's the first new mainline entry since Onimusha: Dawn of Dreams in 2006. The game's release comes with surprisingly modest system requirements.
insight: Revival of a nearly two-decade-old gaming franchise
